fullName: Connection and Use of System Code
authority: Multiparty contract; compliance is a licence condition for NESO, TOs, and users of the transmission system
summary: The commercial and contractual framework for connecting to, and using, the GB national electricity transmission system. CUSC defines how connection offers are made, what gets charged (TNUoS, BSUoS), how Bilateral Connection Agreements work, and — crucially — how the TMO4+ connections reform is implemented through modifications CMP434/CMP435.
scope: Section 2 — Connection process and the CUSC Application Rules
Section 3 — Use of System: TNUoS and BSUoS charging methodologies
Section 4 — Construction Agreement, Bilateral Connection Agreement, Bilateral Embedded Generation Agreement
Section 6 — Balancing Services agreements and MSA (Master Services Agreement)
Section 8 — Modification process, CUSC Panel governance

keyParts.name: Section 2 — Connection
keyParts.detail: Application windows, Gate 1 / Gate 2 offers, securities, acceptance windows (90 days), modification of existing offers.

keyParts.name: Section 3 — TNUoS / BSUoS
keyParts.detail: Zonal transmission charging, locational signals, Balancing Services Use of System socialised recovery.

keyParts.name: Section 4 — Construction and Connection Agreements
keyParts.detail: The bilateral contracts between NESO, the relevant TO and the user.

keyParts.name: Section 11 — Governance
keyParts.detail: CUSC Panel, voting, modification decisions by Ofgem.

keyParts.name: CMP434 / CMP435
keyParts.detail: Implementing TMO4+ — new Application Rules, Gate 1/Gate 2, biannual windows, Ready and Needed criteria.

keyParts.name: CMP422 / CMP423
keyParts.detail: Earlier queue management reforms that preceded TMO4+.
